seoTitle: How to Create a New Jira Ticket Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to create a new Jira ticket, choose the right issue type, assign it to a teammate, and set priority and due dates.
intro: Whether you're reporting a bug or assigning a task, getting the ticket details right from the start makes tracking easier down the line. This guide covers creating a new Jira ticket, choosing the correct issue type, and filling in the details that matter.

steps.heading: Open the create issue window
steps.body: Click Create to open the form used for adding new tickets to your project.

steps.heading: Select the project and issue type
steps.body: Choose the correct project, then select the issue type that matches what you're reporting, such as Bug, Task, or Story.

steps.heading: Fill in the ticket details
steps.body: Write a clear summary and description, and add any attachments needed to help whoever picks up the work.

steps.heading: Assign and submit the ticket
steps.body: Set the assignee, priority, labels, and due date, then submit the ticket to add it to your project.

faq.question: How do I create a new ticket in Jira?
faq.answer: Click Create, select your project and issue type, fill in the ticket details, and click Create to submit it.

faq.question: What issue type should I choose for a bug report?
faq.answer: Select Bug as the issue type so it's tracked separately from standard tasks and stories.

faq.question: Can I attach files when creating a Jira ticket?
faq.answer: Yes, the create issue form includes an attachment option so you can add screenshots or supporting files.

faq.question: How do I set a due date on a new ticket?
faq.answer: The create issue form includes a due date field where you can set a target completion date before submitting.
